2605.23320.title: Human-in-the-Loop Multi-Agent Ventilator Decision Support with Contextual Bandit Preference Learning
2605.23320.paper_summary: Ventilator decision support requires sequential decisions that track evolving physiology and disease trajectories while respecting safety boundaries and clinician specific tuning styles. Rule based approaches rarely generalize personalization, and end to end reinforcement learning or single large language model systems remain difficult to control and audit. We propose the Ventilator Decision Support System (VDSS), a human in the loop multi agent framework that coordinates modular decision components through contract driven structured interfaces and produces traceable evidence for review. VDSS performs online preference adaptation with a contextual bandit, updating clinician specific preferences from the final accepted decision at each adjustment cycle and using them to guide subsequent recommendations. Structured rejection feedback triggers targeted replanning to reduce unproductive iterations and improve interaction stability. Retrospective ICU trajectory replay with expert review indicates higher recommendation acceptability and fewer interaction rounds to reach an acceptable plan, supporting clinically deployable human AI collaboration.
2605.23320.paper_summary_zh: 呼吸器決策支持需要連續的決策，這些決策能夠追蹤不斷變化的生理狀態和疾病軌跡，同時尊重安全邊界和臨床醫生特定的調整風格。基於規則的方法很少能夠普遍化個性化，而端到端的強化學習或單一大型語言模型系統仍然難以控制和審計。我們提出了呼吸器決策支持系統（VDSS），這是一個人機協作的多代理框架，通過合約驅動的結構化接口協調模塊化決策組件，並產生可追溯的證據以供審查。VDSS 使用上下文強盜進行在線偏好適應，從每個調整周期的最終接受決策中更新臨床醫生特定的偏好，並利用它們來指導後續的建議。結構化的拒絕反饋觸發針對性的重新規劃，以減少無效的迭代並改善互動穩定性。回顧性重症監護病房軌跡重播及專家審查顯示，建議的接受度更高，達成可接受計劃所需的互動輪次更少，支持臨床可部署的人機協作。
2605.23320.author: Sijia Li et.al.
2605.23320.authors: Sijia Li, Xiaoyu Tan, Qixing Wang, Weiyi Zhao, Chen Zhan, Teqi Hao, Xuemin Wang, Lei Gu, Roland Eils, Xihe Qiu
